other_observers: Too many to list; First seen and photographed by Connie Kogler
specific_location: Connie Kogler's house in southwest Loveland
description: This was a bright orange bird with two distinctive white wing bars on dark wings. It was somewhat smaller than a robin - it was obviously an oriole. The head, face, underparts, and tail were orange. The malar area was a really bright orange. There was black between the eye and bill which then extended some way down onto the throat. The back was a dull orange with distinct streaks. The eyes were black and the legs gray. The bill was mostly gray, with a small black patch on the mandible, see attached photograph. Experts Chris Wood and A Jamarillo say it is an adult female because the wear on the primaries and tail was uniform, whereas a first-year bird would have had non-uniform wear.Behaviors: Sitting in a pine tree and regularly coming to feed.Call: none heardPlumage: winter
